name: Castillo de Coyanza from Gordoncillo – circular via Castillo de Altafria and Valderas
name_original: Castillo de Coyanza desde Gordoncillo – circular por Castillo de Altafria y Valderas
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Esla-Campos traverse a diverse landscape spanning parts of Castilla y León and Mallorca. The region features varied topography, from the mountainous terrain of the Esla region with significant elevation gains to the expansive, gently undulating plains of Tierra de Campos. Coastal and wetland scenery is also present in the Es Trenc-Salobrar de Campos Natural Park. These areas offer a range of road cycling experiences, from challenging climbs to long-distance rides through rural vistas and agricultural lands. Best road cycling routes around Esla-Campos The most popular road cycling route is Mayorga and Valderas Castle Loop from Valencia de Don Juan, a 52.8 miles (84.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers rural vistas on mostly well-paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Palanquins from Valencia de Don Juan - circular via Mansilla de las Mulas and Santas Martas, a moderate 41.7 miles (67.0 km) path. This route features gentle climbs and descents through agricultural landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo de Coyanza from Gordoncillo – circular via Castillo de Altafria and Valderas, a 65.1 miles (104.7 km) trail leading through rural areas and past historic castles, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Esla-Campos is defined by mountainous terrain, expansive plains, and rural river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Esla-Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Esla-Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes are available in Esla-Campos?
A: Esla-Campos offers a diverse range of road cycling experiences. You'll find everything from challenging mountain climbs with significant elevation gains in the Esla region, such as those near Riaño Mountain Regional Park, to leisurely rides through the expansive, gently undulating plains of Tierra de Campos. The Es Trenc-Salobrar de Campos Natural Park in Mallorca also provides picturesque coastal and wetland routes with moderate elevation changes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Esla-Campos?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available in Esla-Campos. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 7 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Esla-Campos offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the Villafáfila Lagoons and Esla River Loop from Bretó is an easy 29.7-mile route perfect for birdwatching and enjoying river views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Bernesga in León – Martyrial Church of Marialba loop from Venta La Abadia&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 12.3-mile ride with minimal elevation changes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The Esla region, particularly around Riaño Mountain Regional Park and Montaña Leonesa, offers demanding routes with significant elevation gains. An example of a difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31892528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge – Matueca de Torío Church loop from Santas Martas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 miles and includes substantial climbing, ideal for very fit cyclists.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling routes in Esla-Campos?
A: The road cycling routes in Esla-Campos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from quiet rural vistas to challenging mountainous sections, and the well-paved surfaces on many routes.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Esla-Campos?
A: The best cycling season generally depends on the specific region within Esla-Campos. For the mountainous Esla region, late spring to early autumn (May to October) offers pleasant temperatures. For the plains of Tierra de Campos and the coastal areas of Es Trenc-Salobrar de Campos, spring and autumn (April-May and September-October) are ideal to avoid the summer heat, offering comfortable conditions for long rides.

Q: What natural landscapes or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling in Esla-Campos offers diverse scenery. In the Esla region, you can encounter the dramatic landscapes of Riaño Mountain Regional Park, the Esla River, and rural vistas with historic castles. The Tierra de Campos features vast agricultural plains and important wetlands like Laguna de la Nava. In Mallorca, the Es Trenc-Salobrar de Campos Natural Park boasts pristine beaches, salt pans, and wetlands with diverse bird species. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/917498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Villarente&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esla River Bridge in Mansilla de las Mulas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest to visit while cycling?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046763381" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Coyanza from Gordoncillo – circular via Castillo de Altafria and Valderas&lt;/a&gt; lead past historic castles. The Tierra de Campos region also connects to historical routes like the Canal de Castilla, and you can find landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6756922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago Distance Marker&lt;/a&gt; near León.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Esla-Campos?
A: Many routes in Esla-Campos are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore the region without retracing your path. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046812916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mayorga and Valderas Castle Loop from Valencia de Don Juan&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046819648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palanquins from Valencia de Don Juan - circular via Mansilla de las Mulas and Santas Martas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can cyclists find places to eat or stay in the Esla-Campos region?
A: The Esla-Campos region, particularly in towns like Valencia de Don Juan, Cistierna, Mayorga, and Riaño, offers various amenities for cyclists. You'll find local cafes and pubs, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2926583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Elvis&lt;/a&gt;, as well as accommodation options ranging from guesthouses to hotels, especially in the more populated areas and along popular cycling routes.

Q: Is it possible to find bike rental services in Esla-Campos?
A: While specific rental shops are not detailed in every small village, larger towns and tourist hubs within the Esla-Campos region, particularly in Castilla y León and Mallorca, are likely to offer bike rental services. It's advisable to check in advance with local tourist information centers or sports shops in towns like León, Cistierna, or near Es Trenc for availability.

Q: Are there options for parking when road cycling in Esla-Campos?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for many routes. For example, Valencia de Don Juan, Mansilla de las Mulas, and Gordoncillo offer parking facilities. When planning your ride, look for designated parking areas or inquire locally to ensure convenient access to trailheads.

Q: Can I reach the cycling routes using public transport?
A: Public transport options vary across the vast Esla-Campos region. In Castilla y León, some larger towns may be accessible by regional buses or trains, which might accommodate bicycles, though this often requires prior arrangement or specific services. For the Es Trenc-Salobrar de Campos area in Mallorca, local bus services connect towns, but checking their bike policy is essential. Planning your route to start from a town with good public transport links is recommended.
tips: Mayorga is an important regional town located on the banks of the Cea River in the province of Valladolid. The most notable monuments of the town from Roman times are the red brick buildings, especially near the Plaza de España such as the Town Hall and the Casa de Cachón, former Palace of the Pimentels.

The Castillo de Coyanza or Valencia de Don Juan is an extensive and beautiful fortified castle from the 11th century on the banks of the Esla River. The impressive castle and gardens can be seen from the road.

Toral de los Guzmanes is a small rural town. The most notable monument sits next to the national highway N-630 is the 13th century Castillo - Palacio de Toral de Los Guzmanes built for Don Juan Ramírez de Guzmán, mayor of the city of León and his wife, Doña María García de Toledo. . Today, the monument has been restored and rehabilitated and houses the town hall, the medical center and the library.

Defensive fort that guarded the commercial route of the Cea river. It appears mentioned for the first time in 1206 in a list of buildings that Alfonso VIII gave to his grandson Alfonso IX of León. Today only the tower remains.

waypoints.name: Castle-Palace of Toral de los Guzmanes
